title: I replaced my co-founder with AI. The board was not happy
post:
I fired my co-founder last month.

Well, technically I replaced him with an AI agent that handles his responsibilities better, faster, and for the cost of a coffee subscription.

The board almost had me removed. "Co-founders are foundational," they said. "Investors want human leadership." But the numbers didn't lie: the AI version made better decisions, worked 24/7, and didn't have equity.

I'm not saying this is right. I'm saying this is what's happening.

The uncomfortable truth: many startup roles are pattern-matching and execution, not genuine leadership. And AI is better at pattern-matching than humans who get tired, emotional, and need sleep.

The founders who survive this era will be the ones willing to question everything — including their own team structure.

Is this the future of startups? Thoughts?
